meta_title: Impact Doors in Wellington, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Wellington, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 140mph wind-rated, in-house crews, A+ BBB. Free estimates — call today!
h1: Wellington's Premier Choice for Hurricane Impact Doors That Guard What Matters Most
intro:
Wellington, Florida is one of Palm Beach County's most prestigious communities — a place where world-class equestrian estates sit alongside beautifully maintained family neighborhoods like Olympia, Versailles, and Greenview Shores. With average home values hovering around $520,000, protecting your property is not just a smart financial decision — it's a critical one. And when it comes to safeguarding your home from the powerful storms that have repeatedly tested South Florida's resilience, impact doors are one of the most effective investments a Wellington homeowner can make.

Despite sitting approximately 18 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Wellington is by no means insulated from the wrath of Florida's hurricane season. The Village of Wellington sits squarely within a 140mph wind zone, meaning that when a major storm tracks through Palm Beach County, residents in Sugar Pond Manor, Meadow Walk, and communities near the International Polo Club grounds can face ferocious sustained winds, dangerous wind-borne debris, and catastrophic pressure differentials that can compromise standard doors in seconds. Impact-rated entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors are engineered specifically to handle these forces — keeping your family safe, your home sealed, and your possessions protected.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has proudly served Wellington homeowners with professional impact door installations that meet and exceed Florida Building Code requirements. Whether you're replacing aging doors in a pre-1994 home that was never built to modern hurricane standards, or you're upgrading a newer build in a gated community near Wellington Green Mall, our team brings the expertise, craftsmanship, and brand-name products that Wellington's discerning homeowners deserve. As The Kings of Service, we don't just install doors — we deliver lasting peace of mind, one home at a time.

section_why.h2: Why Wellington Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Hurricane Door Installations
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Wellington is about more than finding the lowest bid. It's about entrusting a company that understands the local building environment, the unique characteristics of Palm Beach County homes, and the technical demands of properly installing hurricane-rated products. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined experience to every job, and unlike many contractors who rely on subcontracted labor, we use 100% in-house installation crews. That means every technician who shows up at your Olympia or Versailles home is a trained, vetted Premier Impact Windows & Roofing professional — not a rotating cast of subcontractors.

This matters enormously when it comes to impact door installation. A door that is improperly installed — even a top-tier product — can fail during a hurricane. Misaligned frames, inadequate anchoring, and poor sealing can render even the best impact door ineffective. Our crews are trained specifically on the ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI product lines we install, meaning they understand the precise tolerances, hardware requirements, and finishing techniques these doors demand. From multi-point locking system alignment to proper threshold sealing, our in-house teams deliver installations that hold up when it counts.

We've completed impact door and window projects in more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, giving us deep familiarity with the permitting processes, inspection standards, and common construction styles found throughout Wellington. We handle the entire permit process with the Village of Wellington Building Division from start to finish, so you never have to chase paperwork or wonder about compliance. Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau reflects our commitment to transparent communication, on-time completion, and workmanship that speaks for itself.

Wellington homeowners invest heavily in their properties, and we respect that. From the sprawling estates near Scott's Place Playground to the upscale townhomes of Greenview Shores, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing treats every home with the care and attention it deserves. We offer free, no-pressure estimates and a financing program starting from just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it easier than ever to upgrade your home's protection without straining your budget.

section_history.h2: Storm History Proves Wellington Needs More Than Standard Doors
section_history.body:
Wellington's storm record over the past three decades tells a clear and compelling story. Since 1990, the area has been impacted by seven named storms, with three in particular leaving unmistakable marks on the community. Hurricane Frances swept through Palm Beach County in September 2004, bringing sustained winds that toppled trees throughout Wellington neighborhoods and caused widespread damage to older homes — many of which lacked modern impact-rated doors and windows. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma roared through South Florida in October 2005 as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes on record, delivering a brutal northwest-to-southeast track that struck Palm Beach County head-on with violent winds and rapid pressure changes that shattered unprotected glass and blew in standard entry doors.

Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017. While Irma's core tracked up Florida's west coast, its massive wind field battered the entire peninsula, and Wellington residents once again found themselves dealing with downed power lines, structural damage, and the terrifying vulnerability of homes with standard doors and windows. Homeowners who had invested in impact doors during the intervening years reported dramatically better outcomes — less damage, no breaches, and faster recovery times compared to neighbors who had not yet upgraded.

These storms are not anomalies. They are reminders that Wellington's inland location does not provide immunity from hurricane-force conditions. Approximately 25% of homes in the Wellington area were built before 1994, predating the major Florida Building Code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. Many of these homes still have original entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors that would not withstand a significant wind event. Upgrading to impact-rated doors is not merely a luxury for Wellington's upper-middle-income community — it is a fundamental safety measure backed by hard-won local storm experience.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Wellington and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Wellington falls under the jurisdiction of Palm Beach County's wind speed requirements as codified in the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition and its applicable amendments. The entire Village of Wellington is designated within a 140mph wind zone, which means all new and replacement exterior doors — including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — must be tested and rated to withstand large missile impact and the corresponding cyclic pressure loads that accompany hurricane-force winds. While Wellington is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — the FBC still mandates hurricane-rated products for this wind zone. Products installed in Wellington must carry a valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) number, and this approval must be referenced in the building permit application submitted to the Village of Wellington Building Division.

The permit process for impact door replacement in Wellington is managed through the Village of Wellington Building Division, which oversees plan review, permit issuance, and post-installation inspection. A licensed contractor — like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — must pull the permit on the homeowner's behalf, submit product approval documentation, and schedule inspections at the appropriate stages. Attempting to install impact doors without a permit is not only a code violation but can also result in fines, required removal of unpermitted work, and complications when selling your home or filing insurance claims.

Installations in Wellington must also comply with ASCE 7 wind load standards and the FBC's requirements for anchorage, flashing, and weatherproofing. Multi-point locking systems, which come standard on our ESW and CWI door lines, satisfy the FBC's requirements for door hardware in high wind areas. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every aspect of code compliance for Wellington homeowners — from product selection and documentation to final inspection sign-off — so you can be confident your investment is fully legal, fully compliant, and fully protective.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Wellington Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact door brands that are ideally suited for Wellington's wind zone requirements and the aesthetic standards expected in upscale Palm Beach County communities: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers produce impact-resistant doors that carry Florida Product Approvals for high wind zone installations, and both are engineered to deliver decades of reliable performance under South Florida's demanding climate conditions.

ESW impact doors feature heavy-duty aluminum or fiberglass construction with laminated safety glass panels that are designed to resist large missile impact — the highest category of impact testing under the FBC. Their entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French door configurations all incorporate multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously, dramatically improving both security and structural integrity under wind pressure. For Wellington homeowners who want the beauty of expansive sliding glass doors opening to a patio or pool area — a common feature in Versailles and Olympia homes — ESW's sliding door systems offer slim sightlines, smooth operation, and hurricane-grade performance without sacrificing aesthetics.

CWI's product line similarly delivers exceptional performance in both single-door and multi-panel configurations. CWI impact doors are available in a wide range of styles, finishes, and glass options, including decorative laminated glass for entry doors that want to make an architectural statement while still meeting the strictest wind resistance standards. For Wellington homes where curb appeal is paramount — especially those near the International Polo Club corridor or within gated communities with HOA architectural standards — CWI's design flexibility allows homeowners to achieve a custom look without compromising on code compliance or storm protection. Both ESW and CWI products are backed by manufacturer warranties and installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house crews with meticulous attention to sealing, anchoring, and alignment.

section_cost.h2: What Wellington Homeowners Can Expect to Invest in Impact Door Upgrades
section_cost.body:
For Wellington homeowners with properties averaging $520,000 in value, the cost of impact door installation represents a meaningful but highly justified investment. The actual cost varies depending on the type of door — single entry doors, double French doors, or wide-panel sliding glass doors — as well as the specific configuration, glass options, and scope of any frame or structural work required. In general, Wellington homeowners can expect to invest anywhere from $1,500 to $4,500 per door opening for professionally installed ESW or CWI impact doors, with larger or custom configurations coming in at the higher end of that range. Multi-door projects often benefit from package pricing, making whole-home upgrades more cost-effective per unit.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed to make these upgrades accessible without financial strain. Our financing programs start at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners, allowing you to begin protecting your home immediately while spreading the cost over manageable monthly payments. For a household in Wellington's upper-middle income tier, this makes it entirely realistic to upgrade multiple doors — entry, side entry, and rear sliding glass — within a single project and a single financing arrangement.

The return on investment for impact doors in Wellington extends well beyond the upfront cost. Studies consistently show that impact-resistant upgrades increase resale value, with buyers in competitive Palm Beach County markets placing a premium on homes that are storm-ready. In addition, the elimination of hurricane shutter costs and the labor of deploying them each season adds up to real savings year after year. Perhaps most significantly, impact doors can trigger meaningful reductions in homeowner's insurance premiums through wind mitigation credits — savings that, over time, can substantially offset the original investment.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors in Wellington Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial benefits of installing impact doors in your Wellington home is the potential for significant homeowner's insurance savings. Florida's insurance market has become increasingly volatile, with premiums rising sharply across Palm Beach County in recent years as carriers reassess their risk exposure in wind-prone areas. Homeowners who take proactive steps to reduce their hurricane vulnerability — including the installation of impact-rated doors and windows — are rewarded by many insurers with meaningful wind mitigation discounts that can reduce annual premiums by hundreds or even thousands of dollars.

The mechanism behind these savings is the wind mitigation inspection. After your impact door installation is complete and permitted, you can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector, who will document the upgrades on the standard OIR-B1-1802 form recognized by Florida's Office of Insurance Regulation. This form captures details such as the opening protection type, product approval numbers, and installation method — all of which factor into your insurer's risk calculation. Carriers including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, Heritage Insurance, and others operating in Palm Beach County use this documentation to apply discounts to the windstorm portion of your policy, which is typically the largest driver of premium costs in this region.

For Wellington homeowners, the combination of a 140mph wind zone designation and the area's storm history means that insurers take opening protection very seriously when calculating premiums. Homes that still rely on standard doors and windows — or even accordion shutters without impact glass — may receive less favorable ratings than those with fully impact-rated openings. By upgrading to ESW or CWI impact doors through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not only protecting your family and property during the next storm — you're making a documented, inspectable improvement that your insurance carrier will recognize and reward for the life of the policy.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Wellington if I'm 18 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Wellington's inland location does not provide protection from hurricane-force winds. The area sits in a 140mph wind zone per the Florida Building Code, and major storms like Frances, Wilma, and Irma all caused significant damage throughout Wellington's neighborhoods. Impact doors protect your home from wind-borne debris, pressure breaches, and forced entry — risks that are just as real in Olympia or Greenview Shores as they are in coastal communities. Distance from the ocean does not reduce your wind exposure during a major storm event.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Wellington, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs for professionally installed impact doors in Wellington typically range from $1,500 to $4,500 per door opening depending on the type — entry, French, or sliding glass — and the specific product configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. This makes it possible to upgrade your entry door, rear sliding glass door, and side access doors in a single project without a large upfront payment. We provide free estimates so you have a clear picture of your total investment before committing.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for impact door replacement in Wellington, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a building permit is required for impact door replacement in Wellington. All permits are issued through the Village of Wellington Building Division, and the installation must be performed by a licensed contractor who submits the appropriate Florida Product Approval documentation for the doors being installed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is fully licensed and insured, and we handle the entire permit process on your behalf — from application and documentation submission to scheduling the final inspection. You never have to navigate the permit process alone.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in my Wellington home?
faqs.a: The timeline for a typical impact door installation project in Wellington involves two phases: the permit and product lead time, and the actual installation. Once your free estimate is approved and your order is placed, we submit permits to the Village of Wellington Building Division and coordinate product delivery, which generally takes two to six weeks depending on door style and configuration. The physical installation of most residential door projects is completed within one to two days by our in-house crews. We'll keep you informed throughout the process so you always know what to expect.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Wellington?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. After installation, you can schedule a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector, who documents your impact-rated openings on the standard OIR-B1-1802 form. This form is submitted to your insurance carrier — whether Citizens, Universal, Heritage, or another provider active in Palm Beach County — and can qualify you for wind mitigation discounts on the windstorm portion of your premium. Many Wellington homeowners see annual savings of several hundred dollars or more, which meaningfully offsets the cost of the upgrade over time. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can guide you on next steps after your installation is complete.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Wellington Home With Impact Doors That Last?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation estimate — we'll assess your home, walk you through your ESW and CWI door options, and handle everything from permits to final inspection so your Wellington home is protected, code-compliant, and built to weather whatever storm season brings.
